notes: JCS preserves supplied Unicode sequences rather than applying NFC
Insignificant source whitespace is not emitted
Whole seconds are emitted with explicit millisecond precision
A permitted string-field change alters canonical bytes
Integer and string representations remain distinct
Identical admitted event semantics with reversed dictionary insertion order
Duplicate object names are rejected before canonicalisation; Duplicate JSON property name: 'a'
Non-JSON NaN token; Non-JSON numeric constant: NaN
Non-JSON Infinity token; Non-JSON numeric constant: Infinity
TE-JCS-1 excludes floating-point values even though base JCS serialises them; Floating-point values are outside TE-JCS-1
Integer exceeds the TE-JCS-1 I-JSON-safe domain; Integer exceeds the I-JSON safe range
Lone Unicode surrogate; Lone Unicode surrogate is not admitted
Precision finer than milliseconds is rejected rather than rounded; Timestamp must use seconds, at most millisecond precision, and Z or a numeric offset
Timestamp without UTC marker or numeric offset; Timestamp must use seconds, at most millisecond precision, and Z or a numeric offset
A fourth fractional digit is rejected even when it is zero; Timestamp must use seconds, at most millisecond precision, and Z or a numeric offset
